comment: 防盗窗顶框小片 (NORTH-authored, 开槽/notched). TWO strips at x4.5-7 and x9-11.5 that AVOID the x7-9 pane core band, so the cap is never coplanar with (nor overlapping) the pane side/post top faces — that internal coplanar pane strip was the real z-fighting source (findings #1), NOT the neighbour boundary. Pulled 1px off the boundary (z starts at 1) and reaching the pane center (z=8) so opposite caps tile along a straight run with no seam. CAVEAT: at L/T/X corners the perpendicular caps still overlap slightly (findings #2) — accepted for the common straight-run case. Multipart rotates (y) per connected side; shown only where the run is capped (TOP). #top = horizontal (顶面) face, #all = vertical (立面) face; cutout.
